title: The regulatory unlock that's reshaping AI infrastructure
excerpt: Alternative cloud networks rise as regulations force AI data sovereignty
content_html:
In 2025 alone, US private AI investment reached $285.9 billion, backing nearly 2,000 newly funded AI companies in a single year. That capital fueled the first era of AI. The harder question now is where organizations actually run these workloads, and under whose legal jurisdiction. The answer to that question is redrawing the global cloud map.

Kevin Cochrane, Chief Marketing Officer at Vultr.

Rather than raw silicon, the next era of AI success is now being determined by which infrastructures can support it best. The markets moving the fastest today aren't necessarily the largest or the wealthiest economies; they are the ones treating AI infrastructure as a mission-critical utility and dismantling the barriers to its deployment.

Regulation and the rise of alternative clouds

Like AI itself, the battle to define the next generation is constantly shifting in approach to combat new challenges and requirements. Whilst the path might once have been to build a centralized mega data-center that you use to serve your global users, shifting regulation and desires for true data sovereignty have, and are, changing that.

As governments scramble to regulate AI development without stifling it, some regions have managed to entangle data center development in years of energy and administrative gridlock. The regions moving faster are treating infrastructure permissions as a competitive asset, enabling neoclouds and alternative cloud providers to build, scale, and operate data centers at a pace the giants will struggle to match.

These alternative networks are already winning enterprise contracts that hyperscalers cannot touch, not because of price, but because of where the data sits and who can legally access it. They are doing so by meeting developers exactly where they are, in environments unaffected by the legacy architecture of traditional hyperscalers.

The compliance problem and "geo-repatriation"

The crux of the problem is jurisdiction. The regions getting ahead are those where operations are not stifled by regulatory gridlock. While the US have led the AI race since its eruption, this very progress is what may now be fueling the regulatory hole that some hyperscalers now find themselves in.

The impending deadlines of the EU AI Act, which have been recently adjusted, and similar global mandates, are triggering a wave of “geo-repatriation”, as organizations realize that housing AI workloads on centralized, US-governed clouds is becoming a compliance liability.

Sign up to the TechRadar Pro newsletter to get all the top news, opinion, features and guidance your business needs to succeed!

For enterprises deploying high-risk systems, compliance requires auditable data governance and human oversight mechanisms. In the EU, the legal and financial damage of non-compliance can trigger fines of up to €35 million or 7% of a company’s global annual turnover.

Faced with these penalties, organizations are realizing that housing AI workloads on centralized, US-governed clouds is a compliance liability. Under the 2018 US Cloud Act, US-based hyperscalers can be compelled to provide US authorities with data stored on their servers, no matter where that data physically resides.

To help handle this friction, enterprises are actively undergoing "geo-repatriation", which is seeing companies move data off US-centric public clouds and transition to region-isolated infrastructure. To avoid regulatory penalties, the models of tomorrow must be trained and deployed on localized networks that offer absolute sovereignty within the borders that they serve.

The great public cloud exodus

As proof of this regulatory pressure, 86% of Chief Information Officers are currently actively planning to migrate at least some workloads away from traditional public clouds. Many are finding the advantages of alternative cloud networks extend well beyond compliance.

The reality is that the legacy hyperscaler architecture was designed for a completely different function than what enterprises now need. As AI usage has developed and increased globally, the regulation and demands of networks have also shifted to match.

For enterprises that are completing complex and constant AI tasks, such as training a Large Language Model, issues can arise when data is bottlenecked by virtual layers and remote servers. To optimize these workloads, enterprises are moving away from traditional public clouds towards alternative cloud providers, who can offer distinct advantages in addition to regulatory compliance:

Bare-metal performance - Traditional, non-regionalized public clouds that run workloads through a hypervisor are costing enterprises performance, speed and money. Alternative cloud providers are offering direct access to bare-metal infrastructure, which, for compute-heavy AI training and inference, has upside over traditional networks.

Decentralized locales - Legacy cloud giants route data through massive regional hubs, resulting in high latency for global users. Alternative cloud networks are deploying agile, high-density data centers in localized regional markets worldwide. This allows enterprises to process data precisely where it is generated, satisfying data-sovereignty mandates and delivering a better experience for users.

Winning the next era of AI

The hyperscalers were built for a world where data could move freely across borders without legal consequence. That world no longer exists. For organizations operating in regulated sectors such as healthcare and finance, contractual promises of security are not sufficient. Data sovereignty must be built into the physical infrastructure, not written into a service agreement.

The next decade of AI infrastructure will be won by providers who built for sovereignty first. Enterprises that recognize this and act before compliance deadlines force their hand will hold a structural advantage over those that do not.

The global AI map is being redrawn. Alternative, sovereign cloud networks built for the realities of modern AI are at the center of that shift, offering enterprises something the hyperscalers cannot: genuine, jurisdictionally enforced control over where their data lives and who can reach it.

We've featured the best AI tool.

This article was produced as part of TechRadar Pro Perspectives, our channel to feature the best and brightest minds in the technology industry today.

The views expressed here are those of the author and are not necessarily those of TechRadarPro or Future plc. If you are interested in contributing find out more here: https://www.techradar.com/pro/perspectives-how-to-submit

Kevin Cochrane, Chief Marketing Officer at Vultr.

sentences.en: The harder question now is where organizations actually run these workloads, and under whose legal jurisdiction.
sentences.zh: 现在更困难的问题是组织实际上在哪里运行这些工作负载，以及在谁的法律管辖范围内。

sentences.en: The answer to that question is redrawing the global cloud map.
sentences.zh: 这个问题的答案是重新绘制全球云图。

sentences.en: The markets moving the fastest today aren't necessarily the largest or the wealthiest economies; they are the ones treating AI infrastructure as a mission-critical utility and dismantling the barriers to its deployment.

sentences.en: Regulation and the rise of alternative clouds

sentences.zh: 虽然曾经的路径可能是建立一个集中式大型数据中心，用于为全球用户提供服务，但不断变化的监管和对真正数据主权的渴望已经并且正在改变这一点。

blocks.html_en: As governments scramble to regulate AI development without stifling it, some regions have managed to entangle data center development in years of energy and administrative gridlock. sentences.zh: 随着各国政府争先恐后地监管人工智能的发展而不扼杀它，一些地区已经使数据中心的发展陷入了多年的能源和行政僵局。

sentences.en: The regions moving faster are treating infrastructure permissions as a competitive asset, enabling neoclouds and alternative cloud providers to build, scale, and operate data centers at a pace the giants will struggle to match.
sentences.zh: 发展速度更快的地区正在将基础设施许可视为一种竞争性资产，使新云和替代云提供商能够以巨头难以匹敌的速度构建、扩展和运营数据中心。

blocks.html_en: These alternative networks are already winning enterprise contracts that hyperscalers cannot touch, not because of price, but because of where the data sits and who can legally access it. sentences.zh: 这些替代网络已经赢得了超大规模企业无法触及的企业合同，不是因为价格，而是因为数据所在的位置以及谁可以合法访问数据。

sentences.en: They are doing so by meeting developers exactly where they are, in environments unaffected by the legacy architecture of traditional hyperscalers.
sentences.zh: 他们通过在不受传统超大规模企业遗留架构影响的环境中准确地与开发人员会面来实现这一目标。

blocks.html_en: The impending deadlines of the EU AI Act, which have been recently adjusted, and similar global mandates, are triggering a wave of “geo-repatriation”, as organizations realize that housing AI workloads on centralized, US-governed clouds is becoming a compliance liability.

sentences.en: The impending deadlines of the EU AI Act, which have been recently adjusted, and similar global mandates, are triggering a wave of “geo-repatriation”, as organizations realize that housing AI workloads on centralized, US-governed clouds is becoming a compliance liability.
sentences.zh: 最近经过调整的欧盟人工智能法案的最后期限即将到来，以及类似的全球指令，正在引发一波“异地遣返”浪潮，因为组织意识到将人工智能工作负载托管在美国管理的集中云上正在成为一种合规责任。

sentences.en: Under the 2018 US Cloud Act, US-based hyperscalers can be compelled to provide US authorities with data stored on their servers, no matter where that data physically resides.
sentences.zh: 根据 2018 年美国云法案，美国的超大规模企业可能被迫向美国当局提供存储在其服务器上的数据，无论这些数据实际位于何处。

blocks.html_en: To help handle this friction, enterprises are actively undergoing "geo-repatriation", which is seeing companies move data off US-centric public clouds and transition to region-isolated infrastructure. sentences.en: To help handle this friction, enterprises are actively undergoing "geo-repatriation", which is seeing companies move data off US-centric public clouds and transition to region-isolated infrastructure.
sentences.zh: 为了帮助解决这种摩擦，企业正在积极进行“异地遣返”，即企业将数据从以美国为中心的公共云转移到与地区隔离的基础设施。

sentences.en: To avoid regulatory penalties, the models of tomorrow must be trained and deployed on localized networks that offer absolute sovereignty within the borders that they serve.
sentences.zh: 为了避免监管处罚，未来的模型必须在本地化网络上进行培训和部署，这些网络在其服务的边界内提供绝对主权。

sentences.en: Alternative cloud providers are offering direct access to bare-metal infrastructure, which, for compute-heavy AI training and inference, has upside over traditional networks.
sentences.zh: 替代云提供商正在提供对裸机基础设施的直接访问，对于计算量大的人工智能训练和推理来说，裸机基础设施比传统网络具有优势。

sentences.en: Alternative cloud networks are deploying agile, high-density data centers in localized regional markets worldwide.
sentences.zh: 替代云网络正在全球本地区域市场部署敏捷、高密度的数据中心。

sentences.en: This allows enterprises to process data precisely where it is generated, satisfying data-sovereignty mandates and delivering a better experience for users.
sentences.zh: 这使得企业能够在数据生成的地方精确处理数据，满足数据主权要求并为用户提供更好的体验。

blocks.html_en: Winning the next era of AI

sentences.en: Winning the next era of AI
sentences.zh: 赢得人工智能的下一个时代

blocks.html_en: The hyperscalers were built for a world where data could move freely across borders without legal consequence. That world no longer exists. For organizations operating in regulated sectors such as healthcare and finance, contractual promises of security are not sufficient. Data sovereignty must be built into the physical infrastructure, not written into a service agreement.

sentences.en: The hyperscalers were built for a world where data could move freely across borders without legal consequence.
sentences.zh: 超大规模数据中心是为一个数据可以自由跨境移动而不会产生法律后果的世界而构建的。

sentences.en: That world no longer exists.
sentences.zh: 那个世界已经不存在了。

sentences.en: For organizations operating in regulated sectors such as healthcare and finance, contractual promises of security are not sufficient.
sentences.zh: 对于在医疗保健和金融等受监管行业运营的组织来说，仅靠合同承诺的安全性是不够的。

sentences.en: Data sovereignty must be built into the physical infrastructure, not written into a service agreement.
sentences.zh: 数据主权必须构建到物理基础设施中，而不是写入服务协议中。

blocks.html_en: The next decade of AI infrastructure will be won by providers who built for sovereignty first. Enterprises that recognize this and act before compliance deadlines force their hand will hold a structural advantage over those that do not.

sentences.en: The next decade of AI infrastructure will be won by providers who built for sovereignty first.
sentences.zh: 未来十年的人工智能基础设施将由那些首先为主权而建设的提供商赢得。

sentences.en: Enterprises that recognize this and act before compliance deadlines force their hand will hold a structural advantage over those that do not.
sentences.zh: 认识到这一点并在合规期限之前采取行动的企业将比那些没有意识到这一点的企业拥有结构性优势。

blocks.html_en: The global AI map is being redrawn. Alternative, sovereign cloud networks built for the realities of modern AI are at the center of that shift, offering enterprises something the hyperscalers cannot: genuine, jurisdictionally enforced control over where their data lives and who can reach it.

sentences.en: The global AI map is being redrawn.
sentences.zh: 全球AI版图正在重新绘制。

sentences.en: Alternative, sovereign cloud networks built for the realities of modern AI are at the center of that shift, offering enterprises something the hyperscalers cannot: genuine, jurisdictionally enforced control over where their data lives and who can reach it.
sentences.zh: 另一种选择是，为现代人工智能的现实而构建的主权云网络是这一转变的核心，它为企业提供了超大规模企业无法提供的东西：对其数据所在位置以及谁可以访问数据进行真正的、司法强制的控制。

sentences.en: We've featured the best AI tool.

blocks.html_en: Kevin Cochrane, Chief Marketing Officer at Vultr.

sentences.en: Kevin Cochrane, Chief Marketing Officer at Vultr.
sentences.zh: Kevin Cochrane，Vultr 首席营销官。

content_html_zh:
仅2025年，美国私人人工智能投资就达到2859亿美元，一年内支持了近2000家新融资的人工智能公司。 这笔资本推动了人工智能的第一个时代。 现在更困难的问题是组织实际上在哪里运行这些工作负载，以及在谁的法律管辖范围内。 这个问题的答案是重新绘制全球云图。

Kevin Cochrane，Vultr 首席营销官。

人工智能的下一个时代的成功现在取决于哪些基础设施能够最好地支持它，而不是原始芯片。 今天变化最快的市场不一定是最大或最富有的经济体；而是那些经济体。他们将人工智能基础设施视为关键任务实用程序，并消除其部署障碍。

监管和替代云的兴起

与人工智能本身一样，定义下一代的斗争也在不断改变方法，以应对新的挑战和要求。 虽然曾经的路径可能是建立一个集中式大型数据中心，用于为全球用户提供服务，但不断变化的监管和对真正数据主权的渴望已经并且正在改变这一点。

随着各国政府争先恐后地监管人工智能的发展而不扼杀它，一些地区已经使数据中心的发展陷入了多年的能源和行政僵局。 发展速度更快的地区正在将基础设施许可视为一种竞争性资产，使新云和替代云提供商能够以巨头难以匹敌的速度构建、扩展和运营数据中心。

这些替代网络已经赢得了超大规模企业无法触及的企业合同，不是因为价格，而是因为数据所在的位置以及谁可以合法访问数据。 他们通过在不受传统超大规模企业遗留架构影响的环境中准确地与开发人员会面来实现这一目标。

合规问题和“异地遣返”

问题的关键在于管辖权。 取得领先的地区是那些运营不受监管僵局阻碍的地区。 尽管美国自人工智能竞赛爆发以来一直处于领先地位，但正是这种进步现在可能加剧了一些超大规模企业现在发现自己陷入的监管漏洞。

最近经过调整的欧盟人工智能法案的最后期限即将到来，以及类似的全球指令，正在引发一波“异地遣返”浪潮，因为组织意识到将人工智能工作负载托管在美国管理的集中云上正在成为一种合规责任。

订阅 TechRadar Pro 时事通讯，获取您的企业成功所需的所有热门新闻、观点、功能和指导！

对于部署高风险系统的企业来说，合规性需要可审计的数据治理和人工监督机制。 在欧盟，违规造成的法律和财务损失可能会引发高达 3500 万欧元的罚款，即公司全球年营业额的 7%。

面对这些处罚，组织逐渐意识到，将人工智能工作负载托管在美国监管的集中云上是一种合规责任。 根据 2018 年美国云法案，美国的超大规模企业可能被迫向美国当局提供存储在其服务器上的数据，无论这些数据实际位于何处。

为了帮助解决这种摩擦，企业正在积极进行“异地遣返”，即企业将数据从以美国为中心的公共云转移到与地区隔离的基础设施。 为了避免监管处罚，未来的模型必须在本地化网络上进行培训和部署，这些网络在其服务的边界内提供绝对主权。

公有云大迁徙

作为这种监管压力的证明，86% 的首席信息官目前正在积极计划将至少部分工作负载从传统公共云迁移出来。 许多人发现替代云网络的优势远远超出了合规性。

现实情况是，传统的超大规模架构的设计目的与企业现在所需的功能完全不同。 随着人工智能在全球范围内的发展和增加，网络的监管和需求也发生了相应的变化。

对于正在完成复杂且持续的人工智能任务（例如训练大型语言模型）的企业来说，当数据受到虚拟层和远程服务器的瓶颈时，可能会出现问题。 为了优化这些工作负载，企业正在从传统的公共云转向替代云提供商，这些提供商除了合规性之外还可以提供独特的优势：

裸机性能 - 通过虚拟机管理程序运行工作负载的传统非区域化公共云正在损害企业的性能、速度和资金。 替代云提供商正在提供对裸机基础设施的直接访问，对于计算量大的人工智能训练和推理来说，裸机基础设施比传统网络具有优势。

分散的区域设置 - 传统云巨头通过大型区域中心路由数据，导致全球用户的高延迟。 替代云网络正在全球本地区域市场部署敏捷、高密度的数据中心。 这使得企业能够在数据生成的地方精确处理数据，满足数据主权要求并为用户提供更好的体验。

赢得人工智能的下一个时代

超大规模数据中心是为一个数据可以自由跨境移动而不会产生法律后果的世界而构建的。 那个世界已经不存在了。 对于在医疗保健和金融等受监管行业运营的组织来说，仅靠合同承诺的安全性是不够的。 数据主权必须构建到物理基础设施中，而不是写入服务协议中。

未来十年的人工智能基础设施将由那些首先为主权而建设的提供商赢得。 认识到这一点并在合规期限之前采取行动的企业将比那些没有意识到这一点的企业拥有结构性优势。

全球AI版图正在重新绘制。 另一种选择是，为现代人工智能的现实而构建的主权云网络是这一转变的核心，它为企业提供了超大规模企业无法提供的东西：对其数据所在位置以及谁可以访问数据进行真正的、司法强制的控制。

我们推出了最好的人工智能工具。

本文是 TechRadar Pro Perspectives 的一部分，这是我们的频道，旨在介绍当今技术行业中最优秀、最聪明的人才。

本文表达的观点仅代表作者的观点，并不一定代表 TechRadarPro 或 Future plc 的观点。 如果您有兴趣贡献，请在此处了解更多信息：https://www.techradar.com/pro/perspectives-how-to-submit

Kevin Cochrane，Vultr 首席营销官。
